_meta.purpose: Curated industry -> distinctive-phrase vocabulary for the lobbying free-text discovery/serving layer (industry_map.py). Fixes cleanup-note B: an industry hidden under many issue codes and vague categories ('lobbying related to taxation') can only be mapped by the VOCABULARY it uses in the free-text, not by issue-code filtering. Each facet's 'phrases' are matched, whole-word and case-insensitively, against lobbying_freetext (senate activity descriptions + house specific_issues) to build the deterministic, cited serving table lobbying_issue_mentions. Same precision-over-recall discipline as bill_aliases.json.
discipline: Precision over recall, same rule as build_db.py's ISSUE_KEYWORDS and bill_aliases.json. Every 'phrases' string is a distinctive, whole-word-matchable term, never a bare topic word.

'phrases' are the ONLY strings used for tagging. 'display_only' terms are recorded (with the reason) but NOT matched: they are either ambiguous (bare 'token', 'mining', 'wallet', 'coin') or catch unrelated filings (bare 'clarity act' matched an athletic-training filing; the distinctive form 'digital asset market clarity' is used instead).

A facet gets its OWN tag, deliberately NOT folded into an ALI issue code. Crypto scatters across 15+ issue codes (FIN/BAN/TAX/SCI/CPI/CDT/AGR/...), only ~40% under FIN, so the ALI code is not the industry. The tag namespace ('CRYPTO') is separate from the ~79 ALI codes.

The vocabulary is versioned and grows by human triage of the discovery loop (freetext_discovery.py: FTS + keyness + quarterly emergence + untagged mining). Never auto-commit a discovered term; a human adds it here with a source, and the serving table is rebuilt. Bump 'version' and note the change when phrases change so a tagged set stays reproducible.

The industry player list emitted from a facet is entity-resolved through lda-entity-resolver (entities/entity_aliases) so it plugs unchanged into ld203_giving.py --names-file (who they give to) and v_client_canonical_spend (what they spend).
_meta.corpus_window: Phrases are calibrated on filings/press 2022-2026 Q1. Bill-name phrases (GENIUS Act, FIT21) are 118th/119th-Congress crypto vehicles; verified distinctive in-window, but re-check when the corpus advances.

facets.label: Cryptocurrency / digital assets

display_only.token: session/tolling/game tokens dominate the bare word; only 'digital token' is kept.
display_only.mining: coal/mineral/data mining (6.7k hits, mostly ALI code NAT); excluded.
display_only.wallet: generic; 'self-hosted wallet' is crypto but low-volume, so wallet is dropped.
display_only.clarity act: bare phrase matched an unrelated athletic-training filing; use 'digital asset market clarity'.
display_only.ripple: 'ripple effect' collision; excluded despite being a crypto firm/coin.

facets.seed_note: The active crypto Enterprise-Map story: an industry hidden under 'taxation' and a dozen other codes, plus diversified filers (Robinhood, PayPal, Fidelity, Stripe, banks) that lobby on crypto without 'crypto' in their name. The vocabulary catches them via what they SAY they lobby on. GENIUS Act (S.1582/S.394, 2025 stablecoin) and FIT21 / the Financial Innovation and Technology for the 21st Century Act (H.R.4763, 118th; market structure) are distinctive crypto bill names and included as phrases; the CLARITY Act appears only via 'digital asset market clarity'.
sources: Vocabulary triaged from freetext_discovery.py keyness/emergence over lobbying_freetext, 2026-07-07
facets.confidence: high on the core terms (crypto/digital asset/stablecoin/blockchain); medium on bill-name phrases (window-specific).
